title: senecio nemorensis subsp. bayonnensis
intro: Senecio nemorensis subsp. bayonnensis, also known as the Pyrenees groundsel or Bayonne groundsel, is a subspecies of the perennial plant Senecio nemorensis. It is a member of the dandelion family and is native to the Pyrenees region between France and Spain. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. Herbal preparations of Senecio nemorensis subsp. bayonnensis have been used to treat a variety of health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions, and are said to have antioxidant and immune-boosting properties.
